claim: Hand A's header recurrence rate of 0.063 (the single miss in H-BV-CIRCA-INSTANS-BENCHMARK-01) is inflated by gallows-initial scribal markers. When gallows-initial characters (plain gallows t, p OR benched-gallows cth, ckh, cph) are stripped from Hand A paragraph-initial tokens and the remaining non-gallows stem is used as the 'header' for recurrence counting, the rate falls at or below 0.055 (Isidore's 0.003 plus the ±0.05 tolerance band). If so, Hand A's header-repetition was a gallows-artifact of scribal convention, not a content-level repetition, and all four CIRCA-INSTANS-BENCHMARK-01 signature measures now match Isidore — the herbal-encyclopedic framework is fully supported. If stripping leaves the rate above 0.055, the gap is genuine and Hand A has an additional structural feature beyond standard medieval encyclopedic prose.
evidence_for: H-BV-CIRCA-INSTANS-BENCHMARK-01 matched Isidore on 3 of 4 measures; the one miss (header recurrence) was only 0.010 above the tolerance band

H-BV-RECIPE-STRUCTURE-01 already documented that the 9 recurring Hand-A paragraph-initial tokens (tor, pchor, tshol, pcheol, tcheody, teol, tcho, tshor, pchocthy) all begin with gallows characters (t or p) — i.e., the recurrence signal IS gallows-driven at the token surface

Brain-V's prior Hand-A work established plain gallows t/p at line-initial as paragraph/section markers (2.8x and 5.0x enrichment); by construction, Hand-A paragraph-initial tokens carry gallows prefixes

If the gallows is a structural marker layered on top of the lexical content (like a capital letter or initial), stripping it recovers the underlying 'header vocabulary' for apples-to-apples comparison with Isidore's lowercase prose
evidence_against: Stripping could INCREASE recurrence if many gallows-prefixed paragraph-initial tokens share a common non-gallows stem (e.g. 'tchor' and 'pchor' both strip to 'chor'). In that case, fewer unique stripped stems but more repetitions per stem — the ratio could go UP, refuting the hypothesis directly.
If gallows prefixes are random decorations on otherwise-unique lexical items, stripping collapses distinct types artificially and creates apparent recurrence where none exists at the content level
The 0.010 tolerance-miss is small; a null stripping effect could plausibly push Hand A across the line via noise rather than genuine signal
test:
LOCKED PROCEDURE on Hand A.

  (1) Re-run the CIRCA-INSTANS-BENCHMARK-01 paragraph extraction (gallows-bounded Hand-A paragraphs, 268 paragraphs from 11,018 tokens used).

  (2) For each paragraph, take position-0 token (paragraph-initial).

  (3) STRIP leading gallows prefix from each paragraph-initial token using longest-match-first rule:
        - if token starts with 'cth', 'ckh', or 'cph' (3-char benched gallows), remove those 3 characters
        - else if token starts with 't' or 'p' (1-char plain gallows), remove that character
        - otherwise, token is unchanged
      The result is a 'stripped header'. Record each paragraph's stripped-header token.

  (4) RECOMPUTE header recurrence on stripped headers:
        n_types = number of distinct stripped-header types
        n_recur = number of stripped-header types appearing as paragraph-initial (stripped) in >=3 paragraphs
        stripped_recurrence = n_recur / n_types

  (5) COMPARISON: Isidore CIRCA-INSTANS value = 0.003. Tolerance band +/- 0.05, so match if stripped_recurrence in [0.00, 0.053] (Isidore + tolerance ≈ 0.053; 0.055 in the user spec is a rounding that we treat as 0.053 to keep the exact +/- 0.05 rule consistent with the locked CIRCA-INSTANS bands).

  (6) REPORT: for audit, list the top-10 stripped-header stems by paragraph count, and the total number of stripped-headers that fell from recurring (>=3) to non-recurring (<3) or vice versa compared to the unstripped version.
test_threshold: CONFIRMED (Hand A's header miss was a gallows artifact; all 4 signature measures now match Isidore within tolerance) if stripped_recurrence <= 0.053. REFUTED (the gap is genuine) if stripped_recurrence > 0.053. Note: stripped_recurrence >= 0.30 is also a REFUTED case (classical-recipe pattern), but this is extremely unlikely given the unstripped rate was 0.063.
tests_remaining: Execute gallows-strip header test in separate commit after lock
reasoning: CIRCA-INSTANS-BENCHMARK-01 matched Isidore on disjunction, top-20 medial, and cross-class rate but missed header recurrence by 0.010. RECIPE-STRUCTURE-01's audit trail shows all nine recurring headers carry plain gallows prefixes, consistent with scribal-marker convention rather than content-level repetition. Stripping that prefix and re-measuring is the clean diagnostic: if gallows-driven, the miss closes; if content-driven, the miss remains and Hand A is known to have an additional structural feature beyond Isidore-type encyclopedia. The test is fast (one-shot script on existing data) and the decision is binding.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-16 user-directed test following CIRCA-INSTANS-BENCHMARK-01 MARGINAL result
